body:
## Verdict

agent_supports: yes
irrlicht_observes: yes

## Reasoning

This scenario is about COEXISTENCE: one Aider session and one different-adapter session (e.g. claudecode or codex) running concurrently in the SAME project directory, each shown with its correct adapter label and fully independent state + metrics. The spec is adapter-agnostic (agentA = first to enter working, agentB = the other), so aider can play either role; a third coexisting adapter is tolerated.

### agent_supports: yes

Aider is an ordinary CLI: an OS `python` process invoking the aider script that edits files in the current working directory. It has no notion of, and imposes no constraint on, what other agent CLIs run in the same cwd. Crucially, aider does NOT write under a shared `$HOME` root — it materializes its chat history per-project in the cwd as `.aider.chat.history.md` (`core/adapters/inbound/agents/aider/agent.go`, `transcriptFilename = ".aider.chat.history.md"`). So launching a second, unrelated agent (claude, codex, pi) in the same directory cannot interfere with a running aider session. The agent trivially "supports" coexistence — there is nothing for it to support or block. The scenario's `requires: [headless_mode]` gate is satisfied (`replaydata/agents/aider/capabilities.json` → `headless_mode: true`).

### irrlicht_observes: yes

The daemon labels each session by its adapter INDEPENDENTLY, by construction — and aider's transport is disjoint from every other adapter's on all three axes:

1. **Per-adapter process matching.** Aider registers `Process.Match = CommandPattern{Regex: /aider}` (`agent.go`) — distinct from the `ExactName` matchers of codex (`codex`), pi (`pi`), and from claudecode's matcher. The process scanner runs every adapter's matcher separately (`core/adapters/inbound/agents/maps.go` → `ProcessNames` / `PIDDiscoverers` projections), so an aider process is tagged `aider` from which matcher hit, never from the cwd. The `/aider` command-pattern anchor specifically excludes wrappers (tmux, sh) that merely mention aider in their argv.

2. **Disjoint transcript transport.** Aider is the only adapter using `FilesUnderCWD{Filename: ".aider.chat.history.md", Parser: RawLineParser}` (`agent.go`), whereas claudecode/codex/pi all use `FilesUnderRoot` under fixed `$HOME`-relative dirs (`~/.claude/projects/`, `~/.codex/sessions/`, `~/.pi/agent/sessions/`). Aider's per-CWD markdown transcript can never collide with another adapter's JSONL-under-root transcript, even when sharing a cwd.

3. **Per-(adapter, session_id) state + metrics.** Each running aider process gets its OWN fresh `Parser` instance (`agent.go`: `NewParser: func() agent.RawParser { return &Parser{} }`) precisely so aider's idle-flush turn-tracking state never collides across concurrent processes. The aider parser emits a clean per-session arc — `user_message` on a `#### ` prompt, `assistant_message` on each `> Tokens:` model-call line, and `turn_done` synthesized by `IdleFlush` after 1500ms quiet — driving only the aider session's working->ready transition. A turn in a coexisting claude/codex session drives a different transcript via a different parser and cannot be attributed to the aider row, and vice versa.

Because each adapter's matcher + watch transport + parser are wired separately off the `allAgents` projections (`maps.go`), independent labeling and metrics fall out of the architecture rather than needing special-case code — the same property the codex/claudecode/pi rows of this scenario already rely on. Aider's only structural difference (FilesUnderCWD markdown vs FilesUnderRoot JSONL) makes its transport MORE disjoint from the others, not less, so the no-cross-contamination guarantee holds at least as strongly.

Aider has no subagents (`capabilities.json` → `subagents: false`; no `SubagentCounter` registered in `maps.go`), so the "no cross-contamination of subagent counts" assertion is trivially satisfied for the aider row.

## Note on recording

This cell has no recording yet. The scenario's `cross_adapter` list in `scenarios.json` is `[claudecode, codex, pi]` and `by_adapter` has rows only for those three — aider is NOT in the launched-driver set that `run-cell-multi.sh` spins up. The single shared recording (workspace `events.jsonl` + per-adapter transcript) already covers claudecode/codex/pi. Adding aider as a coexisting adapter is a recording-rig task (add aider to `cross_adapter[]` and give it a `by_adapter` row + interactive driver invocation), NOT an observability gap — the verdict above is independent of whether a recording exists. Until the rig launches aider, `applicable: yes` here means the cell is eligible to record but the maintainer must extend `run-cell-multi.sh`'s driver set first.
caveats: No aider recording exists for this cell yet: aider is absent from the scenario's cross_adapter[] list ([claudecode, codex, pi]) and from by_adapter, so run-cell-multi.sh does not launch an aider driver. Recording requires extending the rig (add aider to cross_adapter[] + a by_adapter row) — this is a recording-rig gap, not an observability gap.

Independent labeling/metrics rely on the daemon scanning each adapter's process matcher and transcript transport separately. Aider's transport is doubly disjoint (CommandPattern /aider matcher + per-CWD markdown FilesUnderCWD), so the assumption holds strongly; it would only need re-checking if a future adapter shared the /aider command pattern or the .aider.chat.history.md filename — not the case today.

Aider's working->ready arc closes via IdleFlush after a 1500ms quiet window (effectively ~5s given the SessionDetector's staleWorkingRefreshInterval), unlike adapters with an explicit end-of-turn marker. This affects only the timing of aider's own ready transition, not whether the two arcs stay independent.

sources.note: Aider registers Process.Match = CommandPattern{Regex: /aider} and Source = FilesUnderCWD{Filename: .aider.chat.history.md, Parser: RawLineParser with a fresh per-process NewParser} — per-adapter matcher + per-CWD markdown transport, disjoint from every FilesUnderRoot adapter
AdapterName/ProcessName = aider — distinct label and process name from claudecode/codex/pi/opencode

Parser emits user_message / assistant_message / tool_result and synthesizes turn_done via IdleFlush (1500ms) — a clean per-session working->ready arc; comment documents the per-process Parser instance so idle-flush state never collides across concurrent aider processes

ProcessNames / PIDDiscoverers / Parsers / SubagentCounters / MetricsProviders are all per-adapter-name projections of the allAgents slice — independent labeling and metrics fall out of the wiring; aider registers no SubagentCounter
headless_mode: true (the scenario's requires gate is satisfied); subagents: false (no subagent-count cross-contamination possible for the aider row)

multiple-agents-same-workspace: cross_adapter = [claudecode, codex, pi] and by_adapter has rows only for those three; spec is adapter-agnostic (agentA/agentB, third adapter tolerated) so aider is an eligible coexisting adapter but is not yet in the launched-driver set
Peer assessment for the same scenario (codex yes/yes, conf 0.95) — anchors the per-adapter-independence reasoning that this aider assessment generalizes
